Astragalus sclerocladus, also known as 'Tragacantha scleroclada', is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus sclerocladus include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Astragalus sclerocladus include teas, decoctions, and extracts, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
